interactions.summary: A student struggles through the Ugolino passage and then reads the scene where Ugolino hears the prison door nailed shut, sees his sons offer their own bodies, and remains emotionally frozen.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Student reading, with Jiang briefly supplying the word 'nailing.'

interactions.summary: Jiang interprets the sons' offer as a final act of love that Ugolino cannot recognize because betrayal has trained him to see only betrayal; treachery blinds the soul to love and lets it commit the worst evil.
Jiang asks Ivory to read and notes they can skip the Latin; the student acknowledges, and Jiang summarizes that the king of hell is before them.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Brief classroom exchange plus Jiang transition.

speaker_notes.note: These are quoted Dante readings by a student, including a visibility complaint in seg-0025.
The 'can you read' prompt is logistical, not a substantive public question.

claims.claim: Jiang reads Ugolino's sons' offer to be eaten as a final act of love toward their father.
Because Ugolino has organized his world through betrayal, he cannot recognize love when his sons show it and instead consumes them.
Treachery is devastating because it blinds the person to the possibility of love and thereby permits the most evil acts.
At the center of hell, Jiang says Lucifer chews Judas, Brutus, and Cassius; Judas makes sense as betrayer of Jesus, while Brutus and Cassius create a paradox Jiang will explain.

signature_moments.moment: The sons offer love, but the father has become so fluent in betrayal that love appears to him only as food.
signature_moments.source_phrase: he doesn't recognize this act of love
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the most brutal example of Jiang's claim that sin changes perception before it changes action.

signature_moments.moment: Treachery is blindness: it denies the possibility of love and therefore makes the worst evil thinkable.
signature_moments.source_phrase: it blinds you to the possibility of love
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It condenses the episode's moral psychology into a phrase that can carry a public read.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The perceptual damage caused by treachery: the sinner cannot see love even when it is offered.
The deepest Inferno location where Lucifer chews Judas, Brutus, and Cassius, setting up Jiang's paradox about Caesar and divine order.

chronology_notes.note: The 2026-04-29 lecture gives a vivid later formulation of treachery as a perceptual blindness to love.

uncertainty_notes.note: The line 'eat us, than for us to eat you' appears ASR-damaged; the surrounding interpretation clearly treats the sons as offering themselves to the father.
